availableCommands.name: copy-request-id
availableCommands.description: Copy the last request ID to clipboard

availableCommands.name: multi-model-review
availableCommands.description: Pick models (structured question or inline), then parallel Task reviewers (global)

availableCommands.name: simplify
availableCommands.description: Find low-info comments, one-off helpers, perf issues, and reuse opportunities. (global)

availableCommands.name: babysit
availableCommands.description: Keep a PR merge-ready by triaging comments, resolving clear conflicts, and fixing CI in a loop. (builtin skill)

availableCommands.name: create-hook
availableCommands.description: Create Cursor hooks. Use when you want to create a hook, write hooks.json, add hook scripts, or automate behavior around agent events. (builtin skill)

availableCommands.name: create-rule
availableCommands.description: Create Cursor rules for persistent AI guidance. Use when you want to create a rule, add coding standards, set up project conventions, configure file-specific patterns, create RULE.md files, or asks about .cursor/rules/ or AGENTS.md. (builtin skill)

availableCommands.name: create-skill
availableCommands.description: Create Cursor Agent Skills. Use when authoring a new skill or asking about SKILL.md structure. (builtin skill)

availableCommands.name: create-subagent
availableCommands.description: Create custom subagents for specialized AI tasks. Use when you want to create a new type of subagent, set up task-specific agents, configure code reviewers, debuggers, or domain-specific assistants with custom prompts. (builtin skill)

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or skill in this session on a recurring or variable interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). (builtin skill)

availableCommands.name: migrate-to-skills
availableCommands.description: Convert 'Applied intelligently' Cursor rules (.cursor/rules/*.mdc) and slash commands (.cursor/commands/*.md) to Agent Skills format (.cursor/skills/). Use when you want to migrate rules or commands to skills, convert .mdc rules to SKILL.md format, or consolidate commands into the skills directory. (builtin skill)

availableCommands.name: sdk
availableCommands.description: Guide users building apps, scripts, CI pipelines, or automations on top of the Cursor SDK - TypeScript (`@cursor/sdk`) or Python (`cursor-sdk` / `cursor_sdk`). Use when the user mentions integrating, installing, or writing code against the Cursor SDK; says `Agent.create`, `Agent.prompt`, `Agent.resume`, `agent.send`, `run.stream`, `run.messages`, `CursorAgentError`, `@cursor/sdk`, `cursor-sdk`, or `cursor_sdk`; asks to run Cursor agents programmatically from a script, CI/CD pipeline, GitHub Action, backend service, or other code outside the Cursor IDE; wants to pick between local and cloud runtime, configure MCP servers for an SDK agent, or handle streaming, cancellation, or errors; or is wiring Cursor into an automation, bot, or REST `/v1/agents` migration. Use eagerly rather than answering from memory; the SDK surface evolves and this skill is the source of truth for the external packages. (builtin skill)

availableCommands.name: shell
availableCommands.description: Runs the rest of a /shell request as a literal shell command. Use only when the user explicitly invokes /shell and wants the following text executed directly in the terminal. (builtin skill)

availableCommands.name: split-to-prs
availableCommands.description: Split current work into small reviewable PRs. Use when the user asks to split a chat, set of changes, branch, or PR. (builtin skill)

availableCommands.name: statusline
availableCommands.description: Configure a custom status line in the CLI. Use when the user mentions status line, statusline, statusLine, CLI status bar, prompt footer customization, or wants to add session context above the prompt. (builtin skill)

availableCommands.name: update-cli-config
availableCommands.description: View and modify Cursor CLI configuration settings in ~/.cursor/cli-config.json. Use when the user wants to change CLI settings, configure permissions, switch approval mode, enable vim mode, toggle display options, configure sandbox, or manage any CLI preferences. (builtin skill)

import json
with open('/tmp/openapi.json') as f:
    d=json.load(f)
tables=[p.strip('/') for p in d.get('paths',{}) if p.startswith('/') and p != '/' and not p.startswith('/rpc')]

for t in sorted(tables):
    print(t)
print('---definitions---')
for name, defn in sorted(d.get('definitions',{}).items()):
    props = defn.get('properties',{})
    print(f'{name}: {list(props.keys())}')
"

`set -a && source /home/csillag/.local/share/optio-cursor/pontusx-demo__analyze_6a4fa7ec5899d8be164da775_cursor/workdir/.env && set +a
for t in financials machines maintenance_logs production_runs products sensor_readings suppliers; do
  count=$(curl -sS -I -H "apikey: $API1_TOKEN" -H "Authorization: Bearer $API1_TOKEN" -H "Prefer: count=exact" "${API1_BASE}/${t}?select=id" | tr -d '\r' | grep -i content-range | sed 's/.*\///')
  echo "$t: $count"
done`

rawOutput.content:
#!/home/csillag/excavator-analysis-tools/bin/python3
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import re
import sys
from recipe_dsl.cli import main
if __name__ == '__main__':
    sys.argv[0] = re.sub(r'(-script\.pyw|\.exe)?$', '', sys.argv[0])
    sys.exit(main())
